title: cuscuta cotijana
intro: Cuscuta cotijana, also known as dodder, is a type of parasitic flowering plant that grows by attaching itself to other plants using its stem, ultimately deriving its nutrients from its host. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Cuscuta cotijana include its ability to reduce swelling and pain, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Cuscuta cotijana can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies to treat various health conditions.
